org.apache.paimon.format.parquet.writer

Class ParquetBulkWriter

  • java.lang.Object
    • org.apache.paimon.format.parquet.writer.ParquetBulkWriter
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    FormatWriter

    public class ParquetBulkWriter
extends Object
implements FormatWriter
    A simple FormatWriter implementation that wraps a ParquetWriter.

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor and Description
      ParquetBulkWriter(org.apache.parquet.hadoop.ParquetWriter<InternalRow> parquetWriter)
      Creates a new ParquetBulkWriter wrapping the given ParquetWriter.

      Finishes the writing.

      Flushes all intermediate buffered data to the output stream.

      Check if the writer has reached the targetSize.

    • Constructor Detail

      • ParquetBulkWriter

        public ParquetBulkWriter(org.apache.parquet.hadoop.ParquetWriter<InternalRow> parquetWriter)
        Creates a new ParquetBulkWriter wrapping the given ParquetWriter.
        Parameters:
        parquetWriter - The ParquetWriter to write to.

    • Method Detail

      • addElement

        public void addElement(InternalRow datum)
                throws IOException
        Description copied from interface: FormatWriter
        Adds an element to the encoder. The encoder may temporarily buffer the element, or immediately write it to the stream.

        It may be that adding this element fills up an internal buffer and causes the encoding and flushing of a batch of internally buffered elements.

        Specified by:
        addElement in interface FormatWriter
        Parameters:
        datum - The element to add.
        Throws:
        IOException - Thrown, if the element cannot be added to the encoder, or if the output stream throws an exception.

      • flush

        public void flush()
        Description copied from interface: FormatWriter
        Flushes all intermediate buffered data to the output stream. It is expected that flushing often may reduce the efficiency of the encoding.
        Specified by:
        flush in interface FormatWriter

      • finish

        public void finish()
            throws IOException
        Description copied from interface: FormatWriter
        Finishes the writing. This must flush all internal buffer, finish encoding, and write footers.

        The writer is not expected to handle any more records via FormatWriter.addElement(InternalRow) after this method is called.

        Important: This method MUST NOT close the stream that the writer writes to. Closing the stream is expected to happen through the invoker of this method afterwards.

        Specified by:
        finish in interface FormatWriter
        Throws:
        IOException - Thrown if the finalization fails.

      • reachTargetSize

        public boolean reachTargetSize(boolean suggestedCheck,
                               long targetSize)
                        throws IOException
        Description copied from interface: FormatWriter
        Check if the writer has reached the targetSize.
        Specified by:
        reachTargetSize in interface FormatWriter
        Parameters:
        suggestedCheck - Whether it needs to be checked, but subclasses can also decide whether to check it themselves.
        targetSize - The size of the target.
        Returns:
        true if the target size was reached, otherwise false.
        Throws:
        IOException - Thrown if calculating the length fails.
